dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a reported GSW at --Address Redacted-- in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Taylor, a paramedic with the rescue squad. [COMPANY_NAME] and RFD are on scene.
She was shot-five gunshots were heard; she collapsed shortly after.
She's unresponsive. Testing motor and verbal responses.
No response to pain or voice. Confirmed in cardiac arrest.
Airway appears patent with no visible obstructions.
Agonal respirations, shallow and slow. Work of breathing is abnormal.
Initial respirations 3 per minute-agonal. Second check at 12 per minute post-cardiac arrest.
Weak carotid pulse-rate is 50 per minute.
No detectable blood pressure-readings 0/0.
SpO2 is 0-no oxygen saturation detected.
Applying direct pressure to multiple wounds-right upper abdomen and left hand.
Pressure applied. Bleeding is significant, especially at the scapula.
Initiating high-flow oxygen via BVM mask and starting compressions.
O2 on at 15 LPM through BVM mask. Providing assisted ventilations.
Initial rhythm is PEA. EKG pads applied for continuous monitoring.
Skin is pale and warm. Edema noted to the left neck, left of the trachea.
Pupils are unreactive. No head or neck trauma otherwise.
Entry wounds to the left hand and a large wound to the right upper abdomen with possible scapular exit.
Exit wound at the scapula remains uncontrolled despite pressure and dressing.
Female, 30 years old. No known medical history, medications, or allergies.
Applying occlusive dressing to scapular wound. Frequent reapplication required.
Bleeding remains uncontrolled. Reapplying dressing as needed.
Capillary glucose measurement not attempted due to lack of circulation.
Establishing intraosseous access at right humeral head for medication delivery.
IO access successful. Readying for epinephrine delivery.
Administering epinephrine 1 mg via IO at 21:35 as per protocol.
Rhythm remains PEA at 21:32. No spontaneous return of circulation.
Performing four-lead ECG monitoring for documentation.
Starting normal saline infusion via IO using pressure infuser for volume support.
Administering second dose of epinephrine at 21:40. CPR ongoing.
Inserting oropharyngeal airway to assist airflow and prevent aspiration.
OPA inserted successfully. Airway remains patent with good compliance.
ETCO2 reading is 14 mmHg-airway secure with equal bilateral breath sounds.
Transporting to [COMPANY_NAME]. Patient unresponsive and in PEA.
Patient loaded onto stretcher-CPR and ventilation ongoing for transport.
Compressions at 100/min with adequate depth and recoil.
Notifying ED en route-trauma bay on standby at [COMPANY_NAME].
Delaying on scene for equipment decontamination due to heavy blood exposure.
Administering third dose of epinephrine at 21:43. PEA remains unresolved.
Inserting King LT airway for secured airway. No obstruction found.
King airway inserted-good chest rise and equal breath sounds bilaterally.
Activating emergency stretcher transfer-preparing patient for ambulance.
Arrived at [COMPANY_NAME]. Patient will be transferred to trauma bay.
Puts established due to ongoing cardiac arrest. Full care transitioned to trauma team.
Unresponsive 30-year-old female with GSWs to left hand and right abdomen. PEA with active CPR.
Bleeding remains uncontrolled at the scapular exit wound. IO established, epinephrine administered.
Contusion noted on left side of the neck. All equipment decontaminated before transport.
